guardrails: 6 of the 8 defect-mask classes are fixed by bilinear-error invariants; the 2 remaining splits need the mask-transport (canonical mask-count) profile.
This is a structural decomposition of the column classification, not an atlas-independent theorem; the 15/49 realization is item 7.
No public record-level widening or universal-24 claim is made.

metadata.description: Structural decomposition of the 8 B3 defect-mask classes into a bilinear-determined part (6 classes) and a mask-transport residue (2 further splits).

source_artifacts: development provenance not shipped: private B3 record-level support-action source

summary.next_task: Proceed to item 6 (the 13 NF1/NF2/NF3 clauses) and item 7 (the 15/49 matrix as an abstract theorem).
summary.reading: The intrinsic key (defect, factorization orientation, pair/triple relation of error directions to the selected signature) -- all bilinear-error data from Steps 5+/9+ -- fixes 6 of the 8 defect-mask classes. The two splits {DM01,DM02} (defect 4) and {DM04,DM06} (defect 6) are resolved by the T/K+D4 canonical mask-count profile, equivalently the error support set; these are the mask-transport residue. All eight classes are orbit-pure functions of intrinsic (bilinear + mask) invariants.
summary.scope_note: This is NOT atlas-independent like Steps 3-5: the 8 classes classify the actual B3 orbits. It separates the bilinear-fixed part (6/8) from the mask-transport part (2 splits). Which class realizes which signature (the 15/49 matrix) is item 7.
